import re

with open("/mnt/data/file-MXVx39sBbc8bSFi2sqPei5-text_to_analyse.txt", "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    text = f.read()

spans = []

# Pattern 1: Hadith between quotes
for m in re.finditer(r'\"([^\"]*?)\"', text):
    content = m.group(1)
    # Check if this content likely contains an ayah or hadith keyword
    # We'll collect all quoted segments for review
    spans.append(("quoted", content, m.start(1), m.end(1)))

len(spans), spans[:10]

import re

text = open("/mnt/data/file-MXVx39sBbc8bSFi2sqPei5-text_to_analyse.txt","r",encoding="utf-8").read()

def find_verses_before_ref(sura_ref):
    spans = []
    for m in re.finditer(re.escape("{"+sura_ref+"}"), text):
        end_of_verse = m.start()
        # look back up to 300 chars to find start (heuristic): start from "جَن" or "وَال"
        start = max(0, end_of_verse - 300)
        window = text[start:end_of_verse]
        # Find last occurrence of Arabic letter sequence with diacritics that seems start of verse.
        # We'll look for starts with either 'ج' (for جنات عدن) or 'و' (for والذين آمنوا)
        candidates = []
        for pat in ["جَن", "وَال", "وَالَّذِينَ", "جَنَّاتُ", "جَنَّاتُ"]:
            p = window.rfind(pat)
            if p != -1:
                candidates.append(start+p)
        if not candidates:
            continue
        verse_start = max(candidates)  # nearest
        versestring = text[verse_start:end_of_verse].strip()
        # Also strip trailing punctuation / spaces
        versestring = versestring.strip()
        spans.append((versestring, verse_start, verse_start+len(versestring)))
    return spans

spans_ref1 = find_verses_before_ref("الرعد:23")
spans_ref2 = find_verses_before_ref("الطَّوْرِ:21")
spans_ref1, spans_ref2

def find_verses_before_ref_precise(sura_ref):
    spans = []
    for m in re.finditer(re.escape("{"+sura_ref+"}"), text):
        end = m.start()
        start = max(0, end-400)
        window = text[start:end]
        # Find last "جَنَّاتُ عَدْنٍ" occurrence
        idx = max(window.rfind("جَنَّاتُ عَدْنٍ"), window.rfind("جَنَّاتُ عَدْنٍ"))
        if idx != -1:
            verse_start = start + idx
            versestring = text[verse_start:end].rstrip()
            versestring = versestring.rstrip()
            versestring = versestring.rstrip()  # keep as is
            spans.append((versestring, verse_start, verse_start+len(versestring)))
        else:
            # Maybe it's an At-Tur verse; find last "وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا"
            idx2 = max(window.rfind("وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا"), window.rfind("وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا"))
            if idx2 != -1:
                verse_start = start + idx2
                versestring = text[verse_start:end].rstrip()
                spans.append((versestring, verse_start, verse_start+len(versestring)))
    return spans

spans_ref1 = find_verses_before_ref_precise("الرعد:23")
spans_ref1

import re

matches = []

# Pattern for { ... } [الطَّوْرِ:21]
for m in re.finditer(r'\{([^{}]+)\}\s*\[الطَّوْرِ:21\]', text):
    content = m.group(1)
    start = m.start(1)
    end = m.end(1)
    matches.append(("brace_verse_tur_diac", content, start, end))

# Pattern for [الطور:٢١] (hamza etc removed, digits Arabic-Indic)
for m in re.finditer(r'([^\[\]\{\}\n]+?)\s*\[الطور:٢١\]', text):
    # To avoid capturing too much, ensure content contains Arabic letters and maybe diacritics and spaces, but not punctuation heavy
    content = m.group(1).strip()
    # We'll assume content starts with "وَال" for the verse; filter accordingly
    if content.endswith("رَهِينٌ") or content.startswith("وَال"):
        start = m.start(1) + (len(m.group(1)) - len(content))
        end = start + len(content)
        matches.append(("before_bracket_tur", content, start, end))

matches

def find_verse_before_square_ref(square_label, start_hint_phrase):
    spans = []
    for m in re.finditer(re.escape("["+square_label+"]"), text):
        end = m.start()
        start_window = max(0, end-500)
        window = text[start_window:end]
        idx = max(window.rfind(start_hint_phrase), window.rfind(start_hint_phrase.replace("َّ","َّ")))  # account for diacritic variants
        if idx != -1:
            verse_start = start_window + idx
            verse = text[verse_start:end].strip()
            spans.append((verse, verse_start, verse_start+len(verse)))
    return spans

spans_tur2 = find_verse_before_square_ref("الطور:٢١", "وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا")
spans_tur2

text = open("/mnt/data/file-MXVx39sBbc8bSFi2sqPei5-text_to_analyse.txt","r",encoding="utf-8").read()

start_idx = idx if idx != -1 else idx2
start_idx, text[start_idx:second_quote_end]

import re, unicodedata

text = open("/mnt/data/file-MXVx39sBbc8bSFi2sqPei5-text_to_analyse.txt","r",encoding="utf-8").read()

brace_spans = []
for m in re.finditer(r'\{([^{}]+)\}', text):
    content = m.group(1)
    # Exclude if content looks like "الرعد:23" (has colon and digits)
    if ":" in content or ":" in content or content.strip().endswith("..."):
        # But note the verse itself may include colon? No.
        # Also exlude if content has whitespace only or is 'الطَّوْرِ:21'
        if any(ch.isdigit() for ch in content):
            continue
        # If includes 'الرعد' etc treat as ref; continue
        if "الرعد" in content or "الطور" in content or "الطَّوْرِ" in content:
            continue
    # Keep content that seems Arabic letters/diacritics and spaces
    if re.search(r'[ء-ي]+', content):
        brace_spans.append((content, m.start(1), m.end(1)))

brace_spans[:10], len(brace_spans)

# At-Tur full in [3]

# Deduplicate by (start,end)
spans_list = sorted(set(spans_list), key=lambda x: x[0])
spans_list, len(spans_list)

import json

def build_citation_entry(span_start, span_end, span_text, citation_type, aya_ref=None, hadeeth_ref=None):
    entry = {
        "span_start": span_start,
        "span_end": span_end,
        "span_text": span_text,
        "citation_type": citation_type,
        "is_valid": False,
        "aya_reference": aya_ref if aya_ref else {},
        "hadeeth_reference": hadeeth_ref if hadeeth_ref else {},
        "corrected_text": "خطأ",
        "completely_invalid": True
    }
    return entry

citations = []

# 1) 144-238 (Ar-Ra'd 13:23)

citations.description: List of citations with details, validation, references, and per-citation corrections.

span_start.description: Inclusive start character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_end.description: Exclusive end character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_text.description: The text content of the citation span.

citation_type.description: Type of the citation: either Aya (Quran) or Hadeeth (Prophetic tradition).

is_valid.description: True if the cited Aya or Hadeeth is recognized and valid, false otherwise.

aya_reference.description: Quranic reference details (required if citation_type is Aya; empty object if not applicable).

sura_name.description: Name of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

sura_number.description: Number of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

aya_number.description: Number of the aya (verse) in the Quran.

hadeeth_reference.description: Hadeeth reference details (required if citation_type is Hadeeth; empty object if not applicable).

collection_name.description: Name of the Hadeeth collection in english (e.g. Sahih Bukhari, Muslim).

hadith_number.description: Hadith number within the specified book.

corrected_text.description: The corrected version of the citation text (or the text itself if it was correct in the first place) (e.g. if there are certain words that were invalid this version must have this corrected), or 'خطأ' for completely invalid citations (i.e. no close enough citation can be found to correct the text). If there are close texts in Quran or Hadith - but too far from the citation, this must be considered 'خطأ' and completely_invalid must be true.

completely_invalid.description: Set to true if the citation is completely invalid i.e. there can't be a close enough text to correct it, (corrected_text must be 'خطأ').
